title: Glorious Crunchy French Vegetable Pot Au Feu
description: A pungent and crunchy lacto-vegetarian French dish from Lyon, featuring natural yoghurt and katsuramuki-cut vegetables in a pot-au-feu form, perfect for a cold winter evening.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the katsuramuki-cut carrots, turnips, and leeks, and cook for 5 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the dried thyme and bay leaves, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in 4 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the natural yoghurt and cook for 2 minutes until heated through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
